issues.issue: API description must be present and at least 50 characters for AI readiness
issues.description: API must have a comprehensive description for AI understanding
issues.fixSuggestion: Expand `info.description` to at least 50 characters: scope, main resources, auth model, and links to external docs or portals.

issues.issue: Path item should include a summary for better AI route understanding
issues.description: Path items should have summaries to provide route-level context
issues.fixSuggestion: Set `summary` on the path item to describe the resource or route group before drilling into per-method details.

issues.issue: GET operation should declare pagination parameters (cursor/page and limit) for AI agent iteration over large result sets
issues.description: GET operations should support standard pagination parameters
issues.fixSuggestion: Add query parameters such as `limit` plus `cursor` or `page` (and document defaults and max) on list-style GETs.

issues.issue: Operation 'get' description must be present and at least 30 characters for AI readiness
issues.description: All operations must have a detailed description
issues.fixSuggestion: Write at least 30 characters in `description`: inputs, outcomes, idempotency, and notable errors or side effects.

issues.issue: Operation must have an operationId for AI readiness
issues.description: All operations must have an operationId for AI reference
issues.fixSuggestion: Set a unique `operationId` per operation for stable cross-references in code generators and agent prompts.

issues.issue: Operation should have at least one tag for AI categorization
issues.description: All operations should have tags for AI categorization
issues.fixSuggestion: Add `tags` that reference your global `tags` list so operations group correctly in docs and AI context windows.

issues.issue: Operation must define at least one 4xx error response for AI error handling
issues.description: Operations must define 4xx error responses
issues.fixSuggestion: Document the client and auth failure cases you support (e.g. 400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 429) with bodies agents can parse.

issues.issue: Operation should define at least one 5xx error response for AI error handling
issues.description: Operations should define 5xx error responses
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` (or `502`/`503`/`504` as appropriate) response with a schema for generic or upstream failures.

issues.issue: Response should have an example for AI understanding
issues.description: Success responses should have examples
issues.fixSuggestion: Under each 2xx response media type, add `example` or `examples` for a representative success payload.

issues.issue: GET list operation response schema should include pagination metadata (has_more, next_cursor, or total) so AI agents know when to stop paginating
issues.description: List endpoint responses must include pagination metadata
issues.fixSuggestion: Expose at least one of `has_more`, `next_cursor`/`next_page`, or `total` in the JSON body schema for paginated lists.

issues.issue: Path item should include a summary for better AI route understanding
issues.description: Path items should have summaries to provide route-level context
issues.fixSuggestion: Set `summary` on the path item to describe the resource or route group before drilling into per-method details.

issues.issue: GET operation should declare pagination parameters (cursor/page and limit) for AI agent iteration over large result sets
issues.description: GET operations should support standard pagination parameters
issues.fixSuggestion: Add query parameters such as `limit` plus `cursor` or `page` (and document defaults and max) on list-style GETs.

issues.issue: Operation 'get' description must be present and at least 30 characters for AI readiness
issues.description: All operations must have a detailed description
issues.fixSuggestion: Write at least 30 characters in `description`: inputs, outcomes, idempotency, and notable errors or side effects.

issues.issue: Operation must have an operationId for AI readiness
issues.description: All operations must have an operationId for AI reference
issues.fixSuggestion: Set a unique `operationId` per operation for stable cross-references in code generators and agent prompts.

issues.issue: Operation should have at least one tag for AI categorization
issues.description: All operations should have tags for AI categorization
issues.fixSuggestion: Add `tags` that reference your global `tags` list so operations group correctly in docs and AI context windows.

issues.issue: Parameter '' should have an example for AI understanding
issues.description: Parameters should have examples for AI understanding
issues.fixSuggestion: Add `example` or `examples` on query, path, header, and cookie parameters so agents can send realistic values.

issues.issue: Parameter '' description must be present and at least 15 characters for AI readiness
issues.description: All parameters must have meaningful descriptions
issues.fixSuggestion: Document each parameter: meaning, format, units, valid ranges, defaults, and when it is required — at least 15 characters.

issues.issue: Operation should define at least one 5xx error response for AI error handling
issues.description: Operations should define 5xx error responses
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` (or `502`/`503`/`504` as appropriate) response with a schema for generic or upstream failures.

issues.issue: Response '200' description must be present and at least 20 characters for AI readiness
issues.description: All responses must have meaningful descriptions
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` (≥20 chars) to each response code explaining when it is returned, what the body represents, and what the client should do.

issues.issue: Response should have an example for AI understanding
issues.description: Success responses should have examples
issues.fixSuggestion: Under each 2xx response media type, add `example` or `examples` for a representative success payload.

issues.issue: GET list operation response schema should include pagination metadata (has_more, next_cursor, or total) so AI agents know when to stop paginating
issues.description: List endpoint responses must include pagination metadata
issues.fixSuggestion: Expose at least one of `has_more`, `next_cursor`/`next_page`, or `total` in the JSON body schema for paginated lists.

issues.issue: Error response '[404].content' must define response content so AI agents can parse failures consistently
issues.description: All error responses must define structured payload content
issues.fixSuggestion: For 4xx/5xx responses, add `content` with at least one media type so errors are not undocumented plain text.

issues.issue: Response '404' description must be present and at least 20 characters for AI readiness
issues.description: All responses must have meaningful descriptions
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` (≥20 chars) to each response code explaining when it is returned, what the body represents, and what the client should do.

issues.issue: Declare intended audience of every server by defining servers[0].x-internal as true/false.
issues.description: Servers are required to use vendor extension x-internal set to true or false to explicitly explain the audience for the API, which will be picked up by most documentation tools.
issues.fixSuggestion: On every entry in `servers`, add `x-internal: true` (intranet-only, partners, VPN) or `x-internal: false` (public or external-facing) so tools and consumers know the intended audience.

issues.issue: Operation is missing rate limiting response in responses[429].
issues.description: OWASP API Security recommends defining schemas for all responses, even errors. A HTTP 429 response signals the API client is making too many requests, and will supply information about when to retry so that the client can back off calmly without everything breaking. Defining this response is important not just for documentation, but to empower contract testing to make sure the proper JSON structure is being returned instead of leaking implementation details in backtraces. It also ensures your API/framework/gateway actually has rate limiting set up.
issues.fixSuggestion: For each operation under `paths`, add a `429` response with a `content` entry (e.g. `application/json`) and a schema describing the rate-limit / retry body. Document retry semantics and reference gateway rate limits so clients and tests can rely on a stable contract.

issues.issue: Operation is missing rate limiting response in responses[429].content.
issues.description: OWASP API Security recommends defining schemas for all responses, even errors. A HTTP 429 response signals the API client is making too many requests, and will supply information about when to retry so that the client can back off calmly without everything breaking. Defining this response is important not just for documentation, but to empower contract testing to make sure the proper JSON structure is being returned instead of leaking implementation details in backtraces. It also ensures your API/framework/gateway actually has rate limiting set up.
issues.fixSuggestion: For each operation under `paths`, add a `429` response with a `content` entry (e.g. `application/json`) and a schema describing the rate-limit / retry body. Document retry semantics and reference gateway rate limits so clients and tests can rely on a stable contract.

issues.issue: Operation is missing responses[401].
issues.description: OWASP API Security recommends defining schemas for all responses, even errors. The 401 describes what happens when a request is unauthorized, so its important to define this not just for documentation, but to empower contract testing to make sure the proper JSON structure is being returned instead of leaking implementation details in backtraces.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `401` response to each protected operation, including `content` (e.g. `application/json`) and a schema for the unauthorized error body, matching what your API actually returns.

issues.issue: Operation is missing responses[401].content.
issues.description: OWASP API Security recommends defining schemas for all responses, even errors. The 401 describes what happens when a request is unauthorized, so its important to define this not just for documentation, but to empower contract testing to make sure the proper JSON structure is being returned instead of leaking implementation details in backtraces.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `401` response to each protected operation, including `content` (e.g. `application/json`) and a schema for the unauthorized error body, matching what your API actually returns.

issues.issue: Operation is missing responses[500].
issues.description: OWASP API Security recommends defining schemas for all responses, even errors. The 500 describes what happens when a request fails with an internal server error, so its important to define this not just for documentation, but to empower contract testing to make sure the proper JSON structure is being returned instead of leaking implementation details in backtraces.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` response on each operation with `content` and a schema for a generic server-error payload (avoid stack traces); reuse a shared component if all operations return the same error shape.

issues.issue: Operation is missing responses[500].content.
issues.description: OWASP API Security recommends defining schemas for all responses, even errors. The 500 describes what happens when a request fails with an internal server error, so its important to define this not just for documentation, but to empower contract testing to make sure the proper JSON structure is being returned instead of leaking implementation details in backtraces.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` response on each operation with `content` and a schema for a generic server-error payload (avoid stack traces); reuse a shared component if all operations return the same error shape.

issues.issue: Missing error response of either 400, 422 or 4XX.
issues.description: Carefully define schemas for all the API responses, including either 400, 422 or 4XX responses which describe errors caused by invalid requests.
issues.fixSuggestion: For each operation, document at least one client-error response: `400` or `422` with a body schema, or a shared `4XX` response, so validation failures are part of the contract and testable.

issues.issue: OpenAPI object should have non-empty "tags" array.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add at least one top-level `tags` entry in `info` with `name` (and ideally `description`) to group operations in documentation.

issues.issue: Info object should have "contact" object.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add an `info.contact` object with at least `name` and a reachable `email` or `url` so the API is attributable and supportable.

issues.issue: Info object should have "license" object.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add `info.license` with your SPDX or license name, and include `url` to the full license text when possible.

issues.issue: Resource names should generally be plural
issues.fixSuggestion: Prefer plural collection paths (e.g. `/users`, `/orders`) unless the path is a singleton resource clearly named in your API style guide.

issues.issue: Operation "description" should be present and non-empty string.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a non-empty `description` to each operation summarizing behavior, side effects, idempotency, and error cases.

issues.issue: Operation should have "operationId".
issues.fixSuggestion: Assign a unique, stable `operationId` per operation (e.g. `getUserById`) for codegen and client method names.

issues.issue: Operation should have non-empty "tags" array.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `tags` array to each operation referencing global tag names (e.g. `["Users"]`) so UIs and SDKs can group endpoints.

issues.issue: Operation "description" should be present and non-empty string.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a non-empty `description` to each operation summarizing behavior, side effects, idempotency, and error cases.

issues.issue: Operation should have "operationId".
issues.fixSuggestion: Assign a unique, stable `operationId` per operation (e.g. `getUserById`) for codegen and client method names.

issues.issue: Operation should have non-empty "tags" array.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add a `tags` array to each operation referencing global tag names (e.g. `["Users"]`) so UIs and SDKs can group endpoints.

issues.issue: Path parameters should be defined on the path level.
issues.fixSuggestion: Move `in: path` parameters from individual operations to the path item’s `parameters` so all methods share the same path template.
